methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Osmo, a Series B consumer tech company founded by Alex Wiltschko, has raised $130M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of publicly traded comparables and identifiable private peer transactions in the available dataset, limiting the comp universe to zero public and zero private reference points.

Given these constraints, a stage-progression methodology provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised of $130M as a baseline value proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression to subsequent funding rounds yields an implied valuation of $247M. This method assumes Osmo follows conventional venture capital markup patterns at the Series B stage.

The triangulated valuation range spans $210M (low) to $284M (high), with $247M as the median. This 35% range around the median reflects inherent uncertainty given reliance on a single methodology. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier to Osmo's specific market dynamics and capital efficiency, the representativeness of $130M raised as a valuation proxy absent disclosed post-money terms, and the lack of sector-specific or peer-based anchors. Additional comparable transactions or disclosed contemporaneous funding events would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
